import java.util.function.Supplier;

// A lone `throw e;` inside a lambda, as the catch body's sole top-level
// statement, must not read as JV004's lone-rethrow shape - that shape is
// `throw <caught>;` at the top level of the catch, not nested in a lambda.
// (Genuinely swallowed at the top level, so JV001 still fires.)
class LambdaBoundaryUseless {
    void m() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV001"
            Supplier<Integer> s = () -> {
                throw e;
            };
        }
    }

    void risky() throws Exception {}
}
